commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=14db3ebdc4d51a049b0268f2c7441c75d1feca88)
commands.review_summary:
This PR changes ACP final-mode TTS dispatch to defer visible-text settlement for Telegram, attach accumulated text to the synthesized voice payload, preserve audio-only supplements for non-caption channels, and add dispatch tests.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main settles visible text before final TTS, and the live ACP projector queues block text before finalization. I did not establish a live Telegram reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs author or maintainer revision plus redacted Telegram proof; automation should not repair this while contributor-supplied real behavior proof is still missing.

target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, `finalizeAcpTurnOutput()` calls `settleVisibleText()` before TTS synthesis. This flushes the accumulated text to the channel, then the voice note arrives as a separate supplement — causing delete-and-resend churn on Telegram and other surfaces.

This PR defers `settleVisibleText()` until after the TTS outcome is known, and includes the visible text in the TTS payload so Telegram can deliver it as a voice-note caption.

## Root cause

```
Current main flow:
  1. settleVisibleText()     → text message sent to Telegram
  2. maybeApplyTtsToPayload  → voice note synthesized
  3. deliver(voice + supplement) → voice note sent separately
  Result: user sees text, then voice note, then text deleted = churn

Fixed flow:
  1. Skip settleVisibleText()
  2. maybeApplyTtsToPayload  → voice note synthesized
  3. deliver(voice + text as caption) → one combined message
  4. (If TTS fails → settleVisibleText() fallback)
  Result: user sees one voice note with caption = no churn
```

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `dispatch-acp.ts` | Defer `settleVisibleText()` when `ttsMode === "final"` + include text in TTS payload + fallback on failure |
| `dispatch-acp.test.ts` | 3 new tests: deferred settlement, fallback on failure, immediate settlement for non-final |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ auto-reply (50 tests) 33ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  50 passed (50)
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `settleVisibleText()` called before TTS synthesis in `finalizeAcpTurnOutput()`, causing text-before-audio churn.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0. Telegram bot `@thomas_fu_bot` running with `streaming.mode: "off"`.

### Source-level proof

```
$ grep -n "settleVisibleText" src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts

Main branch:
  218: await params.delivery.settleVisibleText();   ← BEFORE TTS, always
  (no fallback path)

Fix branch:
  240: await params.delivery.settleVisibleText();   ← only when NOT doing final TTS
  280: await params.delivery.settleVisibleText();   ← fallback when TTS fails
```

Main branch unconditionally settles text at line 218 before TTS synthesis (line ~239). The fix moves settlement behind a `willAttemptFinalTts` gate and adds a fallback path (line 280) for TTS failure.

### Key design: text included in voice payload

```diff
-            {
+            {
+              text: accumulatedVisibleBlockText,  // ← enables Telegram caption
               mediaUrl: ttsSyntheticReply.mediaUrl,
               audioAsVoice: ttsSyntheticReply.audioAsVoice,
-            },
-            { visibleTextAlreadyDelivered: true },  // ← removed: text is now delivered WITH audio
```

Telegram's `sendVoice` natively supports captions (`splitTelegramCaption()` already exists). By including `text` in the payload and removing `visibleTextAlreadyDelivered`, the channel layer delivers one combined voice+caption message.

- **What was not tested**: Live Telegram TTS session (requires TTS provider credentials and a live conversation). The settlement deferral logic is validated by 50 unit tests covering the final-mode, fallback, and non-final paths.

Closes #83511
